content.body: Hi all, just a friendly reminder to post any discussion topics for this Thursday's joint CSSWG/WHATWG/OpenUI task force meeting to the meeting agenda issue: https://github.com/whatwg/html/issues/12455
headingoffset stuff is indeed a bit annoying from performance point of view.
In the HTML spec, do `this` and `this` mean different things?
content.formatted_body: In the HTML spec, do <b>this</b> and <span>this</span> mean different things?

content.body: jmdyck: you mean in the spec's source? The latter is turned into a link (xref) to a matching `<dfn>`
content.formatted_body: jmdyck: you mean in the spec's source? The latter is turned into a link (xref) to a matching <dfn>

content.body: according to https://webidl.spec.whatwg.org/#this, `this` can be accessed from WebIDL interface getter/setter/method/ctor steps, seems like the this instances are not those
content.formatted_body: according to https://webidl.spec.whatwg.org/#this, <span>this</span> can be accessed from WebIDL interface getter/setter/method/ctor steps, seems like the <b>this</b> instances are not those

content.body: * according to https://webidl.spec.whatwg.org/#this, `this` can be accessed from WebIDL interface member getter/setter/method/ctor steps, seems like the \this\ instances are not those
content.formatted_body: * according to https://webidl.spec.whatwg.org/#this, <span>this</span> can be accessed from WebIDL interface member getter/setter/method/ctor steps, seems like the <b>this</b> instances are not those

m.new_content.body: according to https://webidl.spec.whatwg.org/#this, `this` can be accessed from WebIDL interface member getter/setter/method/ctor steps, seems like the \this\ instances are not those
m.new_content.formatted_body: according to https://webidl.spec.whatwg.org/#this, <span>this</span> can be accessed from WebIDL interface member getter/setter/method/ctor steps, seems like the <b>this</b> instances are not those

content.body: `this binding` I guess is the term defined in ecma262
content.formatted_body: <b>this</b> binding I guess is the term defined in ecma262
